title: Data Catalog Vocabulary (DCAT) Version 3
normative_or_informative_scope: Normative RDF vocabulary and conformance requirements; Section 14 quality guidance is expressly non-normative
conformance_mechanism: Section 4 conformance requirements plus RDF vocabulary semantics; no official closed-world DCAT SHACL profile
maintenance_status: Current W3C Recommendation; supersedes but does not obsolete DCAT 2 and is designed for backward compatibility
limitations: Discovery and catalogue vocabulary with few cardinalities; does not model atomic legal duties or establish metadata truth legal compliance data fitness or quality sufficiency

title: DCAT Application Profile for data portals in Europe
normative_or_informative_scope: Normative application-profile conformance cardinalities controlled-vocabulary requirements and reuse notes; JSON-LD context is non-normative
conformance_mechanism: Release-matched SHACL shapes range shapes controlled-vocabulary shapes and ITB validator; provider and receiver conformance statements also apply
maintenance_status: Current SEMIC Recommendation at the 2026-07-17 check; 3.0.2 exists only as draft
limitations: Profile conformance is context-dependent and does not prove accuracy authority lawfulness fitness or compliance; no native fields for all Data Act uncertainty/API-QoS or AI Act data-governance facts

title: HealthDCAT-AP
normative_or_informative_scope: Draft health-sector DCAT-AP profile including conformance rules cardinalities controlled vocabularies and access-tier variants
conformance_mechanism: Separate mandatory and recommended SHACL suites for PUBLIC RESTRICTED and NON_PUBLIC access plus range and controlled-vocabulary shapes
maintenance_status: Current draft profile at the 2026-07-17 check; Release 6 deprecated
limitations: Draft status and month-only publication date; some examples expose non-canonical w3id links while prefix table and SHACL use http://healthdataportal.eu/ns/health#; quality annotation does not define Article 78 metrics and profile conformance is not EHDS compliance

title: PROV-O: The PROV Ontology
normative_or_informative_scope: Normative OWL2 ontology mapping the PROV data model to RDF classes properties and qualified relations
conformance_mechanism: OWL vocabulary semantics and W3C implementation evidence; no official closed-world SHACL profile
limitations: Generic provenance does not prove that an event occurred that a stated agent was responsible or that a lineage record is complete; OWL reasoning does not impose operational evidence cardinalities

title: Data on the Web Best Practices: Data Quality Vocabulary
issuing_body: W3C Data on the Web Best Practices Working Group
normative_or_informative_scope: Vocabulary definitions and examples in a W3C Working Group Note; Section 7 dimension examples are non-normative and the document is not a W3C Recommendation
conformance_mechanism: RDF vocabulary only; no official DQV SHACL cardinality profile
maintenance_status: Stable Working Group Note; no verified successor Recommendation
limitations: Provides a measurement scaffold but no legally sufficient definition of quality no normative dimension or metric catalogue and no cardinalities; domain metrics methods units provenance and acceptance thresholds must be separately defined

title: Open Digital Rights Language (ODRL) Version 2.2
normative_or_informative_scope: Normative information model and common vocabulary for policies permissions prohibitions duties parties assets actions and constraints
conformance_mechanism: Model conformance and processor semantics; normative JSON-LD context; no official general-purpose SHACL conformance profile
limitations: Expresses a supplied policy rather than governing-law authority validity monitoring or enforcement; domain semantics require a declared ODRL Profile and unrecognised terms may not be interoperable

title: Shapes Constraint Language (SHACL)
normative_or_informative_scope: Normative SHACL Core validation language and optional SHACL-SPARQL extension with standard validation-report vocabulary
conformance_mechanism: Conforming SHACL processor produces sh:ValidationReport against an explicit data graph shapes graph and entailment regime; W3C test suite available
maintenance_status: Stable Recommendation used by this project; SHACL 1.2 Core dated 2026-06-02 remains a Working Draft and is not substituted
limitations: Conformance is only to selected shapes data graph entailment and processor; severity does not change conformance semantics; recursive shapes are undefined and SPARQL constraints may reduce portability; no legal truth or compliance inference follows

title: Machine Learning DCAT Application Profile
normative_or_informative_scope: Candidate application-profile classes properties cardinalities and references for ML models datasets distributions quality measures and related resources; JSON-LD context is non-normative
conformance_mechanism: Release-matched SHACL plus ITB base and role-specific overlay modes
maintenance_status: Current Candidate Recommendation at the 2026-07-17 check; monitor transition or replacement before final thesis claims
limitations: Not an AI Act conformity standard; many legally material Article 10 concepts are optional free-text properties or absent; mandatory quality-measurement linkage does not define metrics methods thresholds representativeness safeguards or substantive sufficiency

title: Croissant Format Specification
normative_or_informative_scope: Community specification for JSON-LD dataset metadata resources record sets fields sources transformations splits labels provenance and usage conditions
conformance_mechanism: Dataset-level dct:conformsTo to the version IRI plus reference implementation/verifier; recommended JSON-LD context is embedded in Appendix 1
limitations: Structural and loading metadata do not prove provenance ownership licence authority quality representativeness or legal compliance; external-vocabulary semantics remain consumer interpreted and the located MLDCAT mapping repository does not cover 3.1.0

title: Croissant Responsible AI Specification
normative_or_informative_scope: Community vocabulary extension documenting dataset lifecycle annotation safety fairness limitations and selected compliance-oriented information
conformance_mechanism: Dataset-level dct:conformsTo to the RAI version IRI; no independent normative SHACL suite identified
maintenance_status: Published extension retained as the verified RAI release at the check date
limitations: Properties are predominantly narrative text with recommended values rather than legal metrics or safeguards; does not establish Article 10 compliance bias mitigation representativeness completeness accuracy or special-category-data necessity

title: ELI Metadata Ontology
issuing_body: ELI Task Force / Publications Office of the European Union
normative_or_informative_scope: Current core OWL ontology release for legislation works expressions formats subdivisions identifiers dates classifications and relationships
conformance_mechanism: OWL ontology semantics; no release-matched SHACL suite relied upon in this project
maintenance_status: Latest core ELI ontology release identified by the official release history at the 2026-07-18 correction check; do not infer a core 1.5 release from the separately versioned ELI-EP profile
limitations: Legal-resource identification and bibliographic structure only; does not represent a complete atomic norm its modality applicability exceptions evidence or compliance conclusion; keep core ELI 1.3 distinct from ELI-EP 1.5

title: ELI-EP Application Profile
normative_or_informative_scope: REGISTRY_ONLY_DEFERRED_FOR_CURRENT_CALIBRATION — Institution-specific SHACL application profile for European Parliament documents activities processes and related entities using ELI ELI-DL and EPVOC
conformance_mechanism: Published SHACL serialization defines profile cardinalities and constraints
maintenance_status: REGISTRY_ONLY_DEFERRED_FOR_CURRENT_CALIBRATION — Version 1.5 created 2022-11-24 and last updated 2023-12-05; separately maintained from core ELI 1.3; admit elements only when an EP-specific capability enters scope
limitations: Deferred from current element mapping because no EP-specific calibration capability is active; European Parliament-specific document and procedure constraints cannot be attributed to the core ELI ontology or generalised to all EU legislation; the profile version 1.5 is not a core ELI ontology version
